title: viola × krascheninnikoviorum
intro: Viola × krascheninnikoviorum, also known as Krascheninnikov's viola or the viola × krascheninnikoviorum, is a hybrid of the European yellow violet (Viola lutea) and the Siberian violet (Viola sibirica), a type of flowering plant in the viola family. Viola × krascheninnikoviorum has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and anthocyanin content. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Viola × krascheninnikoviorum is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
